title: Annal Gandhi Memorial Award
description: Award for Adi Dravidar Hindu students securing 1st rank in 12th Public Examinations.
eligibility: Hindu AD students who secured first rank in 12th Std. Public Exams in their district
benefits: Recognition award; no income limit

title: Adi Dravidar & Tribal Welfare Scholarship (Free Education)
description: Free education up to 12th standard for eligible students.
eligibility: Adi Dravidar/Tribal/Adi Dravidar Converted to Christianity students; no income limit
benefits: Free education up to 12th Std.

title: Post-Matric Scholarship Scheme
description: Financial assistance for students from class XI to XII to promote higher education among backward communities.
eligibility: Parents' income ≤ Rs. 1,00,000; student from Most Backward Classes and Denotified Communities
benefits: Tuition fee of Rs. 500 (Rs. 50 per month for 10 months) and full examination fee coverage for 12th Std.

title: Adi Dravidar & Tribal Welfare Scholarship (Public Exams)
description: Covers examination fees for 10th and 12th Std. students in Adi Dravidar/Tribal communities.
eligibility: Adi Dravidar/Tribal/Adi Dravidar Converted to Christianity students; no income limit
benefits: Public examination fee reimbursed directly to the Directorate of Government Examinations

title: Pragathi Scholarship for Girl Students (Technical Diploma)
description: Scholarship for girl students pursuing technical diplomas, covering tuition, examination, and other incidental expenses.
eligibility: Female students from families with income < Rs. 8 Lakh; maximum duration varies depending on entry year
benefits: Up to Rs. 50,000 per annum for three years for Diploma students (first year) and two years for lateral entry

title: Pre-Matric Scholarship Scheme for financially challenged students
description: Aimed at promoting school education among financially challenged students.
eligibility: Parents' income ≤ Rs. 1,00,000; no graduate in the family; students in 6th to 10th Std.
benefits: Tuition fee: Rs. 200 for 6th-8th Std. and Rs. 250 for 9th-10th Std., paid monthly for 10 months

title: Free Education Scholarship for Professional Courses
description: Financial assistance for students pursuing professional courses.
eligibility: Parents' income ≤ Rs. 1,00,000; no graduate in the family; Backward Classes, Most Backward Classes, and Denotified Communities students
benefits: Tuition fee, special fee, and other non-refundable fees paid as prescribed, with full examination fee coverage

title: Awards to Bright Students
description: Financial reward for students excelling in 10th Public Examinations.
eligibility: First two boys and girls in each district from Adi Dravidar, Tribal, or Adi Dravidar Converted to Christianity communities
benefits: Rs. 800 in the first year and Rs. 960 for the next five years

title: Post-Matric Scholarship for Students with Disabilities
description: Assistance including scholarship, book grant, and escort/reader allowance for disabled students continuing education.
eligibility: Students progressing within the same stage of education
benefits: Varies by course and disability

title: Begum Hazrat Mahal National Scholarship for Minority Girls
description: Financial aid for minority community girls for educational expenses, including fees, books, and lodging.
eligibility: Minority community girls (Muslims, Christians, Sikhs, Buddhists, Jains, Parsis) in class IX to XII; family income ≤ Rs. 2 lakh
benefits: Rs. 5,000 each for Class IX & X, Rs. 6,000 each for Class XI & XII
documents: Previous class mark sheet (min. 50%), school verification form

title: Saksham Scholarship for Specially-Abled Students (Degree)
description: Support for differently-abled students in technical degree programs to pursue further education.
eligibility: Differently-abled students with ≥ 40% disability
benefits: Tuition fee of Rs. 30,000, Rs. 2,000 per month for 10 months as incidental charges, and Rs. 30,000 for books/equipment/software
documents: Mark Sheet of standard Xth/XIIth, Aadhar Card, Disability Certificate

title: Scheme for Meritorious Students (Higher Secondary)
description: Financial aid for high-scoring students from government schools to pursue higher education in top private schools.
eligibility: Top 10 students from government high schools in each district with parental income ≤ Rs. 1 lakh
benefits: Financial assistance to study in selected private schools

title: Free Laptop Scheme
description: Government program providing laptops to students with high marks to promote digital literacy and skill development.
eligibility: Students who passed 10th or 12th Std. with ≥ 75% marks
benefits: Free laptop
documents: 10th and 12th Mark Sheet, Residential Proof, Aadhar Card

title: Top Class Education Scheme
description: Financial aid for SC students pursuing post-secondary education in reputable institutions.
eligibility: SC students with family income ≤ Rs. 8 lakh
benefits: Full tuition fee, living expenses, books, stationery, and laptops
documents: Income Certificate, Aadhar Number, Caste Certificate, Parents' Bank Details

title: National Overseas Scholarship for Scheduled Castes
description: Scholarship for low-income SC students to pursue Master's or Ph.D. programs abroad.
eligibility: SC students with family income less than Rs. 8 lakh; minimum 60% marks in qualifying exam
benefits: Up to 100 awards annually; 30% reserved for women

title: Merit Cum Means Scholarship (Technical Courses for Minorities)
description: Financial aid for minority students pursuing professional/technical courses.
eligibility: Minority students (Muslims, Christians, Sikhs, Buddhists, Jains, Parsis) in class IX-XII with family income ≤ Rs. 2 lakh
benefits: Rs. 5,000 for Class IX & X, Rs. 6,000 for Class XI & XII
documents: School verification form, previous class mark sheet (min. 50%)

title: Free Education Scheme (Degree)
description: Free education for Backward, Most Backward, and Denotified students in 3-year UG courses in government and aided arts/science colleges.
eligibility: Backward, Most Backward, Denotified students in 3-year degree programs
benefits: Special fee, other non-refundable fees, and full examination fees

title: National Merit Scholarship Scheme
description: Scholarships to meritorious students in class 12th with excellent academic records.
eligibility: Students from Class 12th with high marks in exams
benefits: Financial aid for higher studies

title: AICTE-SWANATH Scholarship (Technical Diploma)
description: Support for orphaned students, COVID-19-affected students, and children of martyred armed forces/paramilitary personnel in technical diploma courses.
eligibility: First- or second-year students in AICTE-approved diploma courses
benefits: Rs. 50,000 annually towards college fees, computers, books, and other essentials
documents: Family income certificate, Disability certificate, Caste Certificate, Aadhar Number

title: Book Bank
description: Book lending scheme for SC/ST students to reduce dropout/failure rates due to lack of textbooks.
eligibility: SC/ST students receiving the Government of India Scholarship
benefits: Access to course-specific books in the library for Medical, Engineering, Law, MBA, Veterinary, Agriculture, and Polytechnic courses

title: Free Education Scholarship (Polytechnic)
description: Free education for eligible backward classes in polytechnic courses.
eligibility: Parents' income ≤ Rs. 1,00,000; no diploma/graduate in family; Backward, Most Backward, and Denotified Communities students
benefits: Tuition fee, special fee, and non-refundable fees as per government guidelines

title: Assistance for Education
description: Financial aid for meritorious students with family income ≤ Rs. 4.5 lakh per annum.
eligibility: Children of Folk Artistes, maximum of 2 per family starting from Class X
benefits: Financial support for day-to-day expenses in higher studies, including hostel fees based on course

title: Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department -Hostels - Special Guides
description: Support for boys/girls studying up to XII Standard
eligibility: All Boys/Girls Boarders studying up to XII Standard
benefits: Hostel Facilities

title: Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department -Incentive / Award of Prizes - District Level
description: Awards for the highest marks in Plus 2 and 10th Std. examinations for students from Adi Dravidar/Tribal communities.
eligibility: One Boy and One Girl each from Adi Dravidar/Tribal / Adi Dravidar Converted to Christianity who have secured highest marks
benefits: Incentives of Rs. 3000/- (Plus 2), Rs. 1000/- (10th Std First Prize), Rs. 500/- (Second Prize), Rs. 300/- (Third Prize)

title: Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department - Stationary - Text Books
description: Free textbooks for students studying in Adi Dravidar and Tribal welfare schools.
eligibility: All students studying in 1st standard to 12th standard in Adi Dravidar and Tribal welfare schools
benefits: Free textbooks for students

title: Admission of Adi Dravidar / Tribal students in Plus One at Reputed Schools
description: Admission for outstanding students to reputed private schools for higher secondary education.
eligibility: Students who secure the highest marks in X standard from Adi Dravidar/Tribal Welfare schools
benefits: Financial support for admission to reputed private schools

title: Chief Minister Merit Award
description: Award for students from Adi Dravidar, Tribal, and Adi Dravidar converted to Christianity communities.
eligibility: Top 1000 boys and 1000 girls from the Adi Dravidar, Tribal, and Adi Dravidar Converted to Christianity communities
benefits: Rs. 3000 per annum for 5 years, provided they continue education

title: Chief Minister’s Merit Award
description: Merit-based scholarship for students who secure top marks in the 12th Std. Public Examination and continue higher studies.
eligibility: First 1000 boys and girls who pass the 12th Std. Public Examination and continue their studies
benefits: Rs. 1500 per annum for 5 years

title: DISTRICT LEVEL PRIZES PUBLIC EXAMINATION 10th and 12th Std.
description: Prizes for the best-performing students in 10th and 12th Std. Public Examinations.
eligibility: Top students from Backward, Most Backward, and Denotified Communities
benefits: First Prize: Rs. 6000, Second Prize: Rs. 4000, Third Prize: Rs. 2000

title: Free Education Scheme
description: Free education for 3-year U.G. degree courses for students from Backward, Most Backward, and Denotified Communities.
eligibility: Students in 3-year U.G. degree courses in Government and Government Aided Arts and Science Colleges
benefits: Tuition fees, special fees, and other non-refundable fees paid as prescribed by the government

title: Free Education Scholarship for Polytechnic Courses
description: Free education for students from Backward, Most Backward, and Denotified Communities in 3-year Diploma courses.
eligibility: Students from Backward, Most Backward, and Denotified Communities in Government and Government Aided Polytechnics
benefits: Tuition fees, special fees, and other non-refundable fees paid as prescribed by the government

title: Postmatric Scholarship
description: Scholarship for students studying in 11th and 12th Standard in English Medium.
eligibility: Students from Backward, Most Backward, and Denotified Communities with family income less than Rs. 1,00,000
benefits: Scholarship for tuition, examination, and other prescribed fees

title: Scheme for Meritorious Students to Pursue Higher Secondary Education
description: Financial assistance for students who top their 10th Public examination to pursue higher secondary education in top private schools in Tamil Nadu.
eligibility: Top students from Government High Schools in 10th Std. Public Examinations
benefits: Financial support for admission to reputed private schools

title: Adi Dravidar and Tribal Welfare Department - Incentive / Award of Prizes
description: Incentive and award of prizes for students from Adi Dravidar/Tribal/Adi Dravidar Converted to Christianity communities based on their performance in Plus 2 and 10th Std. examinations.
eligibility: One student each from Adi Dravidar/Tribal/Adi Dravidar Converted to Christianity for each subject in Plus 2 and 10th Std. examinations
benefits: Rs. 2000/- for each subject in Plus 2 Examination, Rs. 1000/- for 10th Std. Examination

title: ICMR- Post Doctoral Fellowship (ICMR-PDF)
description: ICMR-PDF Scheme is instituted to foster high-quality research opportunities for promising fresh Ph.D./MD/MS holders in basic science, communicable and non-communicable diseases, and reproductive health including nutrition at ICMR Institutes/Centers. The fellowship aims to support fundamental research in cutting-edge areas of science and other priority fields identified by ICMR.
eligibility: Indian nationals only; Fresh PhDs/MD/MS within three years of completing their degree (awarded) as on the last date of the application; Proposed research work should continue the candidate's Ph.D. or MD/MS research work; Maximum age limit is 32 years, relaxable up to 5 years for SC/ST/OBC/PH and women candidates; Deserving candidates may receive an additional 3 years' relaxation based on prior work, publications, and awards.
benefits: ₹65,000 per month plus HRA as per government rules and NPA as admissible; Contingency grant of ₹3,00,000 per annum (25% can be used for national travel related to research work); Accommodation may be provided at ICMR Institutes/Centers, but HRA will not be paid in such cases; Medical benefits and leave as per ICMR rules for JRF/Research Associates.
documents: Passport-size Photograph; Attested copy of School Leaving/High School Certificate for date of birth; Attested copies of Mark Sheets/Grade Cards & Degrees; Caste certificate, if applicable; Reprints of publications; One-page summary of Ph.D. thesis; Attested copies of Letter(s) of Acceptance for paper(s) accepted for publication; Detailed research proposal; Testimonials from two referees (one should be the Ph.D. Guide); Plagiarism Check Report verified by ICMR-RIO.

title: Dr. Ambedakar Centrally Sponsored Scheme of Post-Matric Scholarships for the Economically Backward Class (EBC) Students
description: The objective of the scheme is to provide financial assistance to Economically Backward Class (EBC) students studying at the post-matriculation or post-secondary stage in India. Scholarships are awarded by the Government of the State/Union Territory to which the applicant belongs. The scheme is designed to enable students to complete their education.
eligibility: Indian nationals belonging to the General Category (other than Schedule Caste, Schedule Tribe, and Other Backward Classes)
Students pursuing recognized post-matriculation or post-secondary courses in Government institutions in India
Students pursuing post-graduate courses in medicine (provided they are not allowed to practice during the course)
Income from all sources of the student's parents/guardians should not exceed ₹1,00,000 per annum
Students must not be studying in commercial pilot license or aviation-related courses
Only two boys of the same parents/guardians are eligible to receive the scholarship, though this restriction does not apply to girls

Group A.Description: Degree and Post Graduate level courses including M.Phil., PhD, Post Doctoral research, Engineering, Technology, Medicine, Management, and similar fields.

Group B.Description: Graduate/Post Graduate courses like Pharmacy, Nursing, Mass Communication, Hotel Management, etc.

Group C.Description: General courses leading to a graduate degree like BA, B.Sc., B.Com.

Group D.Description: Non-degree courses like ITI courses, senior secondary certificates, vocational courses, and others.

Additional Benefits.Study Tours: Up to ₹900 per annum for professional and technical courses (based on actual expenditure).
Additional Benefits.Thesis Typing/Printing: Up to ₹1,000 for research scholars (based on recommendation).
Additional Benefits.Fees: Scholarship covers compulsory fees such as enrollment, tuition, library, medical, etc., but excludes refundable deposits.

title: AICTE - Research Promotion Scheme (RPS)
description: The scheme 'AICTE - Research Promotion Scheme (RPS)' was introduced by the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE), Department of Higher Education (DoHE) to promote research in identified thrust areas of technical education. AICTE-approved Technical Institutions/University Departments with relevant PG programs and full-time regular faculty with research experience and publications are eligible to apply.
eligibility: AICTE-approved Technical Institutions/University Departments with relevant PG programs.
Full-time regular faculty with research experience and publications.
Preliminary research facilities should be available in the institute.
One proposal from one PI (Principal Investigator) will be sanctioned at any point in time within the duration of the project.

Maximum number of proposals acceptable per institute (including those sanctioned earlier but not completed) shall be five for Govt. institutes and three for Pvt. institutes. However, the maximum number of proposals acceptable to be approved per institute shall be two for both Govt. and Pvt. institutes.

benefits.Duration of the Project: Three years from the date of receipt of funds in the institute’s account.
Limit of Funding: Non-Recurring 75% and Recurring 25% of the total sanctioned grant.
Disbursement of the Funds: 100% Non-recurring and 50% of the recurring grant of the total sanctioned amount.
50% of the total sanctioned recurring grant only after submission of the Audited Utilization Certificate and all requisite documents.
Notes: The grant will be utilized strictly for the purpose as specified in the sanction letter. Re-appropriation of funds from Recurring to Non-Recurring is allowed but not vice versa.
Equipment(s)/item(s) purchased from out of non-recurring should be as per the specifications sanctioned by AICTE.
Recurring grant can be utilized for items sanctioned by AICTE but not for travel abroad to attend conferences/seminars. Travel expenses within the country for presenting a paper may be covered.
Expenditure incurred towards hiring staff on a contract basis shall be considered only if recommended by Experts and approved by AICTE. No budget can be utilized for hiring permanent staff.

documents: APR, AUC, R&P, Statement of Expenditure on completion of each financial year.
Copies of GFR-19, copies of bills & vouchers, and stock entry register duly attested.

title: Fellowship for Superannuated Faculty Members
description: The 'Fellowship for Superannuated Faculty Members' scheme is a fellowship scheme by the University Grants Commission (UGC), Department of Higher Education, to provide opportunities to superannuated faculty members of Universities/Institutes/Colleges to pursue research in their area(s).
eligibility: Faculty members at the level of Professors/Associate Professors (who are going to superannuate within six months or already superannuated) from the following institutions:
Universities (including constituent and affiliated colleges/institutions) included under Section 2(f) of the UGC Act, 1956, and having valid accreditation from NAAC.
Deemed to be Universities under Section 3 of the UGC Act 1956 and having valid accreditation from NAAC.
Institutions fully funded by Central or State Governments and empowered to award Degrees.
Institutions of National Importance.
Age: Up to 67 years.
The applicant must have successfully supervised the Ph.D. dissertations of 10 full-time candidates, 3 of whom have received their degrees during the preceding 10 years.
The applicant must have handled, as Principal Investigator, at least 3 sponsored research projects funded by national/international agencies.
The applicant shall not hold any administrative responsibility during the tenure of the fellowship, which would be from the date of superannuation.
The applicant and Institutions shall have to provide an undertaking in the application as per Annexure-I.

Tenure and Slots: Tenure: 3 years or up to 70 years, whichever is earlier.
Slots: 100 per annum or as may be decided by the Commission.

documents: Scanned copy of your passport-size photograph and signature (size up to 1MB, format: jpg).
Complete Research proposal (size up to 5 MB) and an abstract (size up to 1MB).
Auto-generated application form (after filling out the form), signed by HoD/Registrar, and uploaded before submission.

Proof of being a single girl child (if applicable) via an affidavit on a ₹ 100/- stamp paper, duly attested by SDM/First Class Magistrate/Tahsildar. If parents are not alive, the affidavit can be submitted by the guardian (size: less than 1MB).

title: AICTE-Margdarshan Scheme
description: The Margdarshan Initiative, introduced by the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE) in 2018, aims to improve the quality of technical education through accreditation by facilitating access to resources and mentorship. The scheme involves Mentor Institutes (MIs) and Mentee Beneficiary Institutes (MBIs) to foster a supportive ecosystem for quality enhancement in AICTE-approved institutes.

For Margdarshan Institute or Mentor Inst: AICTE-approved institutions or Central Funded Institutions (e.g., IITs, NITs, IIITs, IISc, ISERs, IIMs).
AICTE-approved institutions must have valid accreditation for at least 6 months from the proposal submission date.
Institutions with diploma courses must have a minimum of four accredited diploma programs.
Accreditation status for non-engineering programs (e.g., Management, Hotel Management) must be valid for at least 6 months.
For Mentee Beneficiary Institute (MBI): MBIs must not have been enrolled as MIs or mapped with any other MI previously.
None of their programs should have been accredited in the past ten years.

For Margdarshan Institute or Mentor Inst: A token grant-in-aid of ₹ 3,00,000/- per MBI, with a maximum of ₹ 30,00,000/- for mentoring up to 10 MBIs.
Suitable honorarium for staff (Chief Coordinator, supporting persons, etc.) engaged in running the scheme, limited to 4% of the overall grant.
Travel Allowances (TA) and honorarium for internal experts:
- AC 2-tier accommodation/Chair Car for train travel.
- By own car/taxi limited to prevailing government rates.
Local support (lodging, boarding, secretarial support, etc.) to be provided by the host MBI during expert visits.
Note: Overall expenditure shall not exceed the sanctioned grant. No reimbursement for excess expenditure.
For Mentee Beneficiary Institute (MBI): Workshops on accreditation processes, SWOT analysis, and infrastructure project submissions.
FDPs/Workshops on research, consultancy, industrial projects, and faculty motivation.
Institutional visits to MIs or other accredited institutes.
Guest lectures on innovation, start-ups, entrepreneurship, and curriculum modifications.
Benchmarking exercises and mapping of activities with Graduate Aptitudes.
Workshops on AICTE initiatives, NIRF ranking parameters, and Outcome-Based Education (OBE).
Institutional reforms: curricular reforms, student performance evaluation, faculty appraisal, and research interaction.
Quality improvement: student learning, employability, faculty productivity, and satisfaction levels.

title: AICTE - Distinguished Chair Professor Fellowship
description: A Fellowship Scheme by the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE), Ministry of Education, aimed at utilizing the expertise of highly qualified and experienced superannuated professionals who have made exceptional contributions in their respective fields. The fellowship is intended to motivate and inspire students and faculty of AICTE-approved institutes. The fellowship is tenable for a period of three years or until the age of 75 years (extendable till 80 years subject to fitness).
eligibility: The awardee must be a highly qualified superannuated professional in their field.
The awardee should be an acknowledged leader/expert in their field of specialization, with active engagement in research and development.
The awardee should have contributed significantly to the development of society through excellent fieldwork.
benefits: An honorarium of ₹10,000/- per day for full-day interaction with faculty/students of the host institute and nearby institutions, up to a maximum of five days in a single trip.
Actual travel expenses (train/air/car) will be covered.

In Online Mode: Institutions are allowed to host up to 5 online sessions/talks with one Distinguished Chair Professor (DCP) assigned by AICTE. Each session should be a minimum of 2 hours, followed by 1 hour of interaction.

title: CBSE Merit Scholarship Scheme For Single Girl Child
description: A scholarship scheme by the Ministry of Education for meritorious Single Girl Students, who are the only child of their parents, and have passed the CBSE Class 10th Examination with 60% or more marks. The scheme aims to recognize the efforts of parents in promoting education among girls and to provide encouragement to meritorious students. The scholarship is ₹500/- per month, paid for a maximum period of two years through ECS/NEFT. The tuition fee should not exceed ₹1,500/- per month during the academic year, with a maximum 10% increase over the next two years. The scholarship is awarded to Indian Nationals only.
eligibility: The applicant must be the single girl child of her family.
The applicant must have secured 60% or more marks in the CBSE Class 10th Examination and be studying in Class 11th & 12th in a CBSE-affiliated school.
The tuition fee should not exceed ₹1,500/- per month during the academic year, with a maximum 10% increase over the next two years.
The applicant must be an Indian National.
The student must continue her school studies in Classes 11th and 12th.
The applicant must have passed the CBSE Class 10th Examination in 2019.
Note: NRI applicants are also eligible, with a maximum tuition fee of ₹6,000/- per month.

title: Collaborative Research Scheme (CRS)
description: Launched by the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE), the Collaborative Research Scheme (CRS) aims to provide financial support in the form of research grants to young TEQIP (Technical Education Quality Improvement Programme) faculty recruited in TEQIP institutes. The scheme encourages collaboration for research and builds a research community.
eligibility: Proposals must be submitted by a group of at least three faculty members, including one Principal Investigator (PI) from a TEQIP institution and Co-Principal Investigators (Co-PIs) from TEQIP or Non-TEQIP institutions.
One TEQIP faculty from the host institute must be the PI.
Co-PI-1 should preferably be a regular faculty from the host TEQIP institute, belonging to the same department as the PI.
A maximum of two proposals can be submitted by one PI.
Only one proposal per PI will be sanctioned at any given time.
Priority for collaboration should be given to TEQIP mentor institutes, NITs, IITs, or Central Universities in the same state or region.
Additional Co-PIs can be from any institution (TEQIP or Non-TEQIP, India or abroad) or industrial sectors.

Limit of Funding: Recurring: 40%, Non-Recurring: 60% of the total grant.
Institutes must open a separate bank account in a nationalized bank for the grant.
Extra funds are provided only in special cases.
Disbursement of Funds: 25% of the total sanctioned Non-Recurring Grant and 25% of the total sanctioned Recurring Grant are disbursed in the first installment.
The remaining funds are disbursed after submission of the Utilization Certificate, requisite documents, and project review by the expert committee.

title: Swami Vivekananda Single Girl Child Fellowship for Research in Social Sciences
description: A fellowship scheme by the University Grants Commission (UGC) for single girl children pursuing research in Social Sciences. The scheme aims to compensate for the direct costs of higher education for girls who are the only child in their family. It promotes the value of small family norms and encourages the concept of a single girl child in society.
eligibility: The applicant must be a single girl child of her parents pursuing a Ph.D. in Social Sciences in Universities/Colleges/Institutes.
The scheme is not applicable if the family has one son and one daughter.
The applicant must be enrolled in a regular, full-time Ph.D. program in Social Sciences in:
- Universities/Colleges/Institutes included under Section 2(f) and 12(b) of the UGC Act, 1956.
- Deemed to be Universities under Section 3 of the UGC Act, 1956, eligible for UGC grants.
- Central/State Government-funded institutions.
- Institutes of National Importance.
Distance education mode is not covered under the scheme.
Age limit: Up to 40 years for general category and 45 years for SC/ST/OBC/PWD candidates as of the last date of application.
Transgender candidates are eligible as per norms.

Financial Assistance: Fellowship: ₹25,000/- per month for the initial two years; ₹28,000/- per month for the remaining tenure.
Contingency: ₹10,000/- per annum for the initial two years; ₹20,500/- per annum for the remaining tenure.
Escort Reader Assistance: ₹2,000/- per month for Persons with Disabilities (PWD) candidates.
benefits.Slots: The number of slots is decided annually based on eligible applications received through online mode.
Tenure: Five years, effective from 1st April of the selection year or the date of joining, whichever is later.
Fellowship ends upon submission of the Ph.D. thesis or completion of five years, whichever is earlier.
No extension beyond five years is permissible.
HRA: Suitable single-seated hostel accommodation may be provided. In such cases, only hostel fees (excluding mess, electricity, water charges) are covered.
If hostel accommodation is unavailable, rent reimbursement is provided up to the HRA ceiling as per Govt. of India norms.
If the fellow arranges her own accommodation, HRA is provided as per Govt. of India city categorization.
A certificate (Annexure-VI) must be submitted for HRA claims.
benefits.Medical: No separate medical assistance is provided. Fellows may avail of medical facilities available in their institutions.
Leave: Maximum of 30 days of leave per year, in addition to public holidays. No other vacations (summer, winter, etc.) are allowed.
Maternity leave as per Govt. of India norms is available once during the tenure.
Academic leave (without fellowship) is permissible for up to one year for academic/teaching assignments or foreign visits related to research. This period counts towards the tenure.

title: Emeritus Fellowship
description: The Emeritus Fellowship scheme provides an opportunity for superannuated teachers to pursue active research in their respective fields of specialization in Indian Universities/Colleges/Institutions. The scheme is applicable to institutions under Section 2(f) and 12(b) of the UGC Act, 1956, Deemed to be Universities under Section 3 of the UGC Act, 1956, Central/State Government-funded institutions, and Institutes of National Importance.

eligibility.Target Group: Highly qualified and experienced superannuated teachers of recognized universities/colleges/institutions.
Criteria: Eligibility is based on the quality of research and published work during the teacher's service career.
The awardee can work under this scheme up to the age of 70 years or for two years (non-extendable), whichever is earlier.

Contingency Grant: ₹50,000/- per annum for secretarial assistance, travel within the country, stationery, postage, consumables, books, journals, and equipment.
Books, journals, and equipment procured will be the property of the university/college/institute.
Foreign Visit: Limited to once a year with prior approval from the institution/university and UGC.
Other Privileges: Medical facilities as available to university faculty members.

title: Post Doctoral Fellowship to Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe Candidates
description: This scheme is initiated to provide SC/ST candidates with an opportunity to undertake advanced studies and research in Science, Engineering & Technology, Humanities, and Social Sciences in Indian Universities/Institutions/Colleges. It aims to support candidates from deprived sections of society in pursuing postdoctoral research.

eligibility.Target Group: Unemployed SC/ST candidates with a Ph.D. degree and aptitude for research.
Criteria: The candidate must have a doctorate degree in the relevant subject, preferably with published research work. Provisional degree certificates are accepted if the degree is not awarded.
Candidates must belong to the Scheduled Caste/Scheduled Tribe category.
Only Ph.D. awarded unemployed candidates are eligible.
Upper age limit: 50 years for male applicants and 55 years for female applicants as of the last date of application.
Candidates must have 50% marks at the UG level and 55% marks at the PG level.
A No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the proposed research place, duly forwarded by the Head of Department and Registrar/Director/Principal, must be uploaded.
Transgender candidates are eligible as per Govt. of India norms.

Financial Assistance.Revised Rate (w.e.f. 01.12.2014): ₹38,800/- per month for the first 2 years; ₹46,500/- per month from the third year onwards.

Financial Assistance.Pre-revised Rate: 10% of the Post Doctoral Fellowship to the host Institution
₹2,000/- per month (fixed) for physically disabled & blind candidates.
Financial Assistance.Revised Rate: ₹2,000/- per month (fixed) for physically disabled & blind candidates.

House Rent Allowance (HRA): Suitable single-seated hostel accommodation may be provided. In such cases, only hostel fees (excluding mess, electricity, water charges) are covered.
If hostel accommodation is unavailable, rent reimbursement is provided up to the HRA ceiling as per Govt. of India norms.
If the fellow arranges their own accommodation, HRA is provided as per Govt. of India city categorization.
benefits.Medical: No separate medical assistance is provided. Fellows may avail of medical facilities available in their institutions.
Leave: Maximum of 30 days of leave per year, in addition to public holidays. No other vacations (summer, winter, etc.) are allowed.
Maternity/Paternity leave as per Govt. of India rules is available once during the tenure.
Academic leave (without fellowship) is permissible for up to one year for academic/teaching assignments or foreign visits related to research. This period counts towards the tenure.

title: AICTE-Technical University Joint Training Programs for Teachers
description: Launched by the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE), this scheme aims to provide financial assistance to technical universities to conduct training programs for faculty members. The goal is to improve teaching-learning methodologies and prepare faculty for successful careers in technical education.
eligibility: AICTE-approved institutions affiliated with the partner university are eligible to participate.

benefits.Maximum Total Expenditure: ₹3,00,000/- per training program, equally shared by AICTE and the university.
AICTE Contribution: ₹1,50,000/- or actual expenditure, whichever is less, to cover:
- TA/DA/Honorarium to Resource Persons (Honorarium not to exceed 20% of total expenditure).
University Contribution: ₹1,50,000/- or actual expenditure, whichever is less, to cover:
- Honorarium to Program Coordinator (not to exceed 1% of total expenditure).

title: Rajiv Gandhi National Fellowship for Scheduled Caste Candidates
description: The Rajiv Gandhi National Fellowship (RGNF) Scheme for Scheduled Caste is funded by the Ministry of Social Justice & Empowerment. It provides financial assistance to SC candidates pursuing full-time M.Phil. and Ph.D. degrees in Sciences, Humanities, Social Sciences, and Engineering & Technology. There are 2000 slots annually, with 3% reserved for Persons with Disabilities (PwD) belonging to SC categories.

eligibility.Target Group: SC candidates who have passed the post-graduation examination and are pursuing full-time research without JRF of UGC-NET or UGC-CSIR NET.
Criteria: The candidate must belong to the SC category and have passed the Post Graduate Examination.
The candidate must be registered for M.Phil./Ph.D.
Transgender candidates are eligible as per Govt. of India norms.

Duration of Fellowship.Maximum Duration: 2 years or submission of dissertation, whichever is earlier.
5 years from commencement of fellowship or submission of Ph.D. thesis, whichever is earlier.
5 years or submission of Ph.D. thesis, whichever is earlier.

Amount of Fellowship.JRF: ₹25,000/- per month for the initial two years.
Amount of Fellowship.SRF: ₹28,000/- per month for the remaining tenure.

Amount of Fellowship.JRF: ₹10,000/- per annum for the initial two years.
Amount of Fellowship.SRF: ₹20,500/- per annum for the remaining tenure.

Amount of Fellowship.JRF: ₹12,000/- per annum for the initial two years.
Amount of Fellowship.SRF: ₹25,000/- per annum for the remaining tenure.

Amount of Fellowship.Rate: ₹2,000/- per month for physically handicapped & blind candidates.
As per Govt. of India norms.

House Rent Allowance (HRA): Suitable single-seated hostel accommodation may be provided. In such cases, only hostel fees (excluding mess, electricity, water charges) are covered.
If hostel accommodation is unavailable, rent reimbursement is provided up to the HRA ceiling as per Govt. of India norms.
If the fellow arranges their own accommodation, HRA is provided as per Govt. of India city categorization.
A certificate (Annexure-VI) must be submitted for HRA claims.
benefits.Medical: No separate medical assistance is provided. Fellows may avail of medical facilities available in their institutions.
Leave: Maximum of 30 days of leave per year, in addition to public holidays. No other vacations (summer, winter, etc.) are allowed.
Maternity/Paternity leave as per Govt. of India rules is available once during the tenure.
Academic leave (without fellowship) is permissible for up to one year for academic/teaching assignments or foreign visits related to research. This period counts towards the tenure.
If the awardee wishes to apply for another fellowship/paid assignment/examination, they must resign from the RGNF.
All leave must be approved by the University/Institute/College.

title: National Scheme of Apprenticeship Training
description: The National Scheme of Apprenticeship Training, overseen by the Department of Higher Education, Ministry of Education, is managed by four Regional Boards of Apprenticeship/Practical (BOATs/BOPT) in Mumbai, Kanpur, Chennai, and Kolkata. The scheme provides practical training to graduate engineers, diploma holders (Technicians), and 10+2 Vocational pass-outs in approximately 10,000 industrial establishments. It operates under the guidelines of the Central Apprenticeship Council (CAC), a statutory body created under the Apprentices Act, 1961. The primary goal is to bridge the gap in practical experience for fresh graduates and vocational pass-outs. The training duration is one year, with a monthly stipend shared equally between the Central Government and the employer.
eligibility: Graduate Engineers.
Diploma Holders (Technicians).
10+2 Vocational Pass-outs.

benefits.Details: The stipend is shared equally between the Central Government and the employer. The employer initially pays the full stipend and later claims 50% reimbursement from the Central Government through BOATs/BOPT.

title: AICTE e-Shodh Sindhu Scheme (eSS)
description: Launched by the All India Council for Technical Education (AICTE), the e-Shodh Sindhu Scheme (eSS) provides subscriptions to e-Resources such as Web of Science, Scopus, The American Society of Mechanical Engineers (ASME), American Society of Civil Engineers (ASCE), and Bentham Science to institutions. The scheme aims to facilitate access to e-Resources for students, faculty, and researchers, fostering a culture of research and improving the quality of technical education.
eligibility: AICTE-approved Government/Government-aided institutes.
The institute must have a digital library network with internet bandwidth (32 Mbps) and Wi-Fi connectivity, along with one or more static IP addresses.
The proposed coordinator must be a full-time regular faculty member with at least 10 years of teaching and research experience, including publications.
The institute must also identify a co-coordinator with at least 7 years of teaching and research experience.
The institute must have PG courses in:
- Mechanical Engineering (for ASME).
- Civil Engineering (for ASCE).
- Pharmacy (for Bentham Science).
- Any discipline (for Web of Science/Scopus).
benefits: Subscriptions to e-Resources like Web of Science, Scopus, ASME, ASCE, and Bentham Science.
Duration: Initially for one calendar year, renewable annually based on usage and fulfillment of eligibility criteria.

title: Financial Support Scheme
description: Implemented by Delhi University, the Financial Support Scheme aims to provide equity and access to quality education for economically weak students. The scheme offers financial support, including a fee waiver for all components of fees (except examination and hostel fees).
eligibility: The student must be a bonafide student of Delhi University.
The student should be enrolled in any UG/PG degree program at a Department/Institution/Centre of Delhi University.
The student's family income should be less than ₹8,00,000/- per annum.

benefits.Family Income: Less than or equal to ₹4,00,000/- per annum
benefits.Remarks: Actual fee or up to a maximum of ₹10,000/-.
Actual fee or up to a maximum of ₹8,000/-.

title: Pradhan Mantri Uchchatar Shiksha Protsahan (PM-USP) Central Sector Scheme of Scholarship for College and University Students
description: A scholarship scheme by the Department of Higher Education, Ministry of Education, aimed at providing financial assistance to meritorious students from poor families to meet a part of their day-to-day expenses while pursuing higher studies. Scholarships are awarded based on the results of the Higher Secondary/Class 12th Board Examination. A maximum of 82,000 fresh scholarships are provided annually for Graduate/Postgraduate degrees and professional courses like Medical, Engineering, etc.
eligibility: The applicant must be above the 80th percentile of successful candidates in the relevant stream from the respective Board of Examination in Class 12th or equivalent.
The applicant must be pursuing regular degree courses at colleges/institutions recognized by AICTE and respective regulatory bodies.
The gross parental/family income of the applicant should not exceed ₹4,50,000/- per annum.
For renewal, the student must score at least 50% marks in the annual examination and maintain at least 75% attendance.
Students must have a bank account in their name.

Rate of Scholarship: ₹12,000/- per annum at the Graduation level for the first three years of College and University courses.
₹20,000/- per annum at the Post-graduation level.
₹20,000/- per annum in the 4th and 5th year for students pursuing professional courses with a duration of five years or integrated courses.
Students pursuing technical courses like B.Tech/B.Engg. will receive ₹12,000/- per annum for the first three years and ₹20,000/- in the 4th year.
benefits.Mode of Disbursal: The scholarship is disbursed directly into the savings bank accounts of beneficiaries through Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).

title: National Scheme of Incentive to Girls for Secondary Education
description: The National Scheme of Incentive to Girls for Secondary Education aims to promote the enrolment of girls aged 14-18 years at the secondary stage, particularly those who pass class 8th but drop out due to socio-economic reasons. The scheme intends to retain such girls up to class 12th. In 2004-05, the dropout rate for girls from classes 1st to 8th was 50.8%, and from classes 1st to 10th, it was 64%. The scheme addresses the financial constraints faced by parents in educating their girl children.
eligibility: The applicant must be a girl child.
The applicant must belong to Scheduled Caste (SC) or Scheduled Tribe (ST).
The applicant must have passed class 8th from Kasturba Gandhi Balika Vidyalayas (irrespective of SC/ST status).
The applicant must have enrolled in class 9th in State/UT Government, Government-aided, or local body schools in the academic year 2008-09 onwards.

benefits.Incentive: A sum of ₹3,000/- is deposited under a term/fixed deposit in a public sector bank or post office in the name of the eligible girl child. The deposit matures when the girl attains 18 years of age, and no premature withdrawal is allowed.

title: Dr. D.S. Kothari Research Grant for Newly Recruited Faculty Members
description: A research grant scheme by the University Grants Commission (UGC), Department of Higher Education (DoHE), aimed at providing opportunities to newly appointed faculty members of Universities/Institutes/Colleges to pursue research in their respective fields. The scheme is open to Assistant Professors appointed against permanent posts, possessing a Ph.D. degree with at least five research papers published in journals of international repute. The tenure of the grant is 2 years, with 132 slots available annually.
eligibility: Faculty members must be regularly appointed and working in:
- Universities/Institutions included under Section 2(f) of the UGC Act, 1956, with valid NAAC accreditation.
- Deemed-to-be Universities under Section 3 of the UGC Act, 1956, with valid NAAC accreditation.
- Institutions fully funded by Central/State Governments and empowered to award degrees.
- Institutions of National Importance.
The applicant must be a newly appointed Assistant Professor against a permanent post.
The Assistant Professor must possess a Ph.D. degree with at least five research papers published in journals of international repute.
The application must be submitted within two years of joining the post.

Financial Assistance: The grant can be utilized for purchasing minor equipment, consumables, contingencies, fieldwork, travel, etc.
Items/equipment purchased from the grant will be the property of the University.
Notes: The grant cannot be used for international travel, purchasing furniture, or appointing project assistants/research fellows.
The allocation of funds under various heads can be decided by the recipient based on their needs.

title: Post Doctoral Fellowship to Women Candidates
description: The UGC has initiated the 'Post Doctoral Fellowship for Women' to support unemployed women holding Ph.D. degrees in their respective subject areas. The scheme aims to accelerate the research potential of women candidates by providing financial assistance for advanced studies and research. The fellowship duration is five years, with no provision for extension, and 100 slots are available annually.
eligibility: The candidate must be unemployed and hold a Ph.D. degree (Provisional Certificate is acceptable if the degree is not awarded).
The candidate must be 55 years of age as of the last date of application submission. Age relaxation of 5 years is applicable for SC/ST/OBC/PwD candidates.
Minimum marks required:
- General Category: 55% at UG level and 60% at PG level.
- Reserved Categories (SC/ST/OBC/PwD): 50% at UG level and 55% at PG level.
A No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the proposed research center, signed by the HOD and Registrar/Principal/Director, must be uploaded.
Transgender candidates are eligible as per Govt. of India norms.

Financial Assistance.Fellowship: ₹38,800/- per month for the first two years; ₹46,500/- per month thereafter.

Financial Assistance.Escorts/Reader Assistance: ₹2,000/- per month (fixed) for Persons with Disabilities (PwD).

House Rent Allowance (HRA): Suitable single-seated hostel accommodation may be provided. In such cases, only hostel fees (excluding mess, electricity, water charges) are covered.
If hostel accommodation is unavailable, rent reimbursement is provided up to the HRA ceiling as per Govt. of India norms.
If the fellow arranges her own accommodation, HRA is provided as per Govt. of India city categorization.
benefits.Medical: No separate medical assistance is provided. Fellows may avail of medical facilities available in their institutions.
Leave: Maximum of 30 days of leave per year, in addition to public holidays. No other vacations (summer, winter, etc.) are allowed.
Maternity leave is available at full rates of the fellowship for 180 days as per Govt. of India rules, once during the tenure.
Special leave without fellowship and contingency may be granted for up to three months on the recommendation of the Head of the Department.

title: Post Graduate Scholarship for GATE/GPAT Qualified Students for Pursuing M.Tech/M.E./M.Pharm - UGC
description: The University Grants Commission (UGC) implements this scholarship scheme to support GATE/GPAT-qualified students pursuing M.Tech, M.E., M.Pharm, and integrated dual-degree courses. The scheme aims to attract talented graduate students to pursue postgraduate education in higher education institutions.
eligibility: GATE/GPAT-qualified students admitted to M.Tech, M.E., M.Pharm, or integrated dual-degree programs in Central, State, Deemed, or Private Universities.
Students must be admitted as full-time scholars.
Government of India policies for SC/ST/OBC/PwD reservations are strictly followed.

Notes: If a student scores below 60% in any semester, the scholarship is reduced to ₹1,550/- for that semester. If the student reappears and scores above 60%, the full scholarship is restored.
The contingency grant is reimbursed based on actual expenditure. Books and non-consumable items purchased become the property of the institution and must be returned after the program.
No house rent allowance is provided.
Scholars must not undertake any paid assignments during the scholarship tenure.
Scholars are required to undertake 8-10 hours per week of teaching and research-related activities.
Scholarship is terminated if the student fails any semester.

title: Post-Graduate Scholarships for Professional Courses for SC/ST Candidates
description: The scheme provides financial assistance to SC/ST candidates for pursuing postgraduate studies in professional courses such as Engineering & Technology, Management, Pharmacy, etc., in recognized Indian Universities/Institutions/Colleges. The objective is to support 1000 SC/ST candidates annually.
eligibility: The student must belong to the SC/ST category.
The candidate must have a graduate degree in the relevant subject and be admitted to a full-time postgraduate course in a recognized University/Institution/College.
Only first-year postgraduate students in the current academic year are eligible.
Eligible Institutions:
- Universities/Institutions/Colleges included under Section 2(f) and 12(B) of the UGC Act.
- Deemed-to-be Universities under Section 3 of the UGC Act, 1956, eligible for UGC grants.
- Central/State Government-funded institutions.
- Institutes of National Importance.

benefits.Tenure: Two/three years, depending on the duration of the postgraduate course.
benefits.Contingency: No contingency is payable from the selection year 2017-18.
benefits.Payment Start Date: The scholarship is payable from the date of joining the postgraduate course.

title: AICTE – Mitacs Globalink Research Internship (GRI) Scheme
description: AICTE, in collaboration with Mitacs Canada, offers the Globalink Research Internship (GRI) Program to foster cross-border research partnerships between Canada and India. The program is open to students in Science, Technology, Engineering, and Mathematics (STEM) disciplines. The internship duration is 12 weeks, with a maximum of 300 students accepted annually. The total cost of the program is $12,000 CAD per student, with Mitacs contributing $9,000 CAD and AICTE contributing $3,000 CAD. The program covers airfare, visa, medical, stipend, accommodation, and research costs, along with access to professional development activities in Canada.
eligibility: The applicant must be a 3rd-year full-time undergraduate student (B.E/B.Tech) in an AICTE-approved institution.
The applicant must have a minimum CGPA/GPA of 85% in the 2nd year.
The applicant must be at least 18 years old.
The applicant must be available to spend 12 weeks in Canada between May and September for a research project.
The applicant must demonstrate fluency in English or French, depending on the project's primary language.

Coverage: Access to Globalink-branded activities in Canada (e.g., workshops, receptions, industrial engagement).

title: Savitribai Jyotirao Phule Fellowship for Single Girl Child (SJSGC)
description: The fellowship is provided by the University Grants Commission (UGC), Department of Higher Education, for pursuing research work leading to the award of a Ph.D. degree. This fellowship is specifically for the single girl child, i.e., the only girl child in the family without any brother or sister. The scheme is aimed to support the higher education of single girl children in Social Sciences.

eligibility.Single Girl Child: Only the single girl child of the parents
eligibility.Ph.D. Program: Must be enrolled in a regular, full-time Ph.D. program
eligibility.Terms and Conditions: Both the scholar and institution must ensure eligibility and adherence to scheme terms

Tenure.Effective From: 1st April of the selection year or actual date of joining, whichever is later
Tenure.End: Up to the date of submission of the Ph.D. Thesis or 5 years, whichever is earlier

HRA.Hostel Accommodation: Hostel fees excluding mess, electricity, water charges
HRA.Non-Availability of Hostel: Reimbursement for single accommodation rent as per Govt. of India norms
HRA.Own Accommodation: HRA as per Govt. of India norms with certificate from institution

Financial Assistance.Medical Assistance: No separate medical assistance, but medical facilities of the institution can be availed

Leave.Earned Leave: Maximum of 30 days per year in addition to public holidays
Leave.Maternity/Paternity Leave: As per Govt. of India norms for female scholars
Leave.Intermittent Break: Maximum of 1 year, permissible 3 times, no fellowship available during this period
Leave.Academic Leave: Permissible for 1 year, counted towards tenure, no fellowship available

title: Saksham scheme for specially-abled students
description: Saksham is a Scholarship Scheme by the Ministry of Education, implemented by AICTE, aimed at providing encouragement and support to specially-abled children to pursue technical education. It provides financial assistance to specially-abled students enrolled in diploma level courses at AICTE-approved institutions. The scholarship covers various expenses such as college fees, books, equipment, and more, for a maximum of 3 years for first-year students and 2 years for second-year lateral entry students.

eligibility.Admission: Candidate should be admitted to the First year or Second year (lateral entry) of a Diploma level course in AICTE-approved institutions.
eligibility.Disability: Specially-abled students with a disability of not less than 40%
eligibility.Family Income: Family income should not exceed ₹8 lakh per annum during the current financial year (Income certificate required)

benefits.Amount: ₹50,000/- per annum for every year of study
benefits.Payment Mode: Scholarship paid through DBT mode annually
benefits.Renewal: Scholarship renewed upon submission of passing certificate and letter from the Head of the Institution via National Scholarship Portal

title: Coaching for SC/ST/OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) & Minority Communities
description: The scheme provides coaching to students belonging to SC, ST, OBC (non-creamy layer), and minority communities to help bridge the educational gap and enhance their competitiveness in pursuing higher education. The scheme includes remedial coaching, coaching for entry into services, and coaching for NET/SET exams, targeting both undergraduate and postgraduate students.

eligibility.Institutions: Universities and colleges eligible under Section 2(f) and Section 12(B) of the UGC Act, 1956.
Student Criteria: Sufficient number of SC/ST/OBC (Non-Creamy Layer) & Minority Community students
General candidates with BPL cards may also apply.
Up to 40% of OBC (non-creamy layer) and poor general candidates may be considered if sufficient minority students are unavailable.

Non-Recurring Items.Amount: Up to Rs. 5.0 Lakhs for each scheme during the XII Plan period

Recurring Items.Amount: Rs. 7.0 Lakhs per annum for universities
Items: Remuneration for Teachers and Scholars (Rs. 300/hr for theory, Rs. 150-200/hr for practicals)
Part-time Staff with Computer Knowledge: Rs. 6000/month

title: Junior Research Fellowship (JRF) & Research Associateship (RA) for Foreign Students and Teachers
description: The scheme aims to provide an opportunity for foreign students and teachers from developing countries in Asia, Africa, and Latin America to undertake advanced study and research leading to M.Phil/Ph.D. and postdoctoral research in sciences, humanities, and social sciences at Indian Universities.

eligibility.Target Group: Students and teachers from developing countries in Asia, Africa, and Latin America.

Academic Qualification: High-class master’s degree with a second-class bachelor’s degree or
Second-class master’s degree with a first-class bachelor’s degree
Doctorate degree with published research work

Fellowship: Rs. 12,000/- per month for the first two years
Rs. 14,000/- per month for remaining years (subject to committee recommendation)

Contingency.Science: Rs. 12,000/- per annum for the first two years
Contingency.Humanities and Social Sciences: Rs. 10,000/- per annum for the first two years

Other Assistance.HRA: As per the rules of the concerned institution

Research Associateship (RA).Departmental Assistance: 10% of Associateship to the host institution for infrastructural facilities
Research Associateship (RA).HRA: As per the rules of the concerned institution

Accommodation.Hostel: Single-seated hostel accommodation may be provided or HRA as per university rules
Accommodation.Own Accommodation: HRA as per city categorization by Government of India

benefits.Medical: No separate medical assistance, but medical facilities available at institutions/universities.

Leave.Maternity Leave: 135 days for female awardees as per Government of India rules
Leave.Special Leave: Leave without fellowship for up to three months (subject to approval)

title: Dr. D.S. Kothari Post Doctoral Fellowships in Sciences
description: Introduced by the University Grants Commission, Dept. of Higher Education, to provide a flexible Post Doctoral Fellowship scheme with fast-track online handling and decision-making.
eligibility: Ph.D. awarded unemployed candidates in relevant subjects with published research work; upper age limit of 35 years, with relaxation for SC/ST/OBC/Women candidates as per Government of India norms.

HRA: Hostel accommodation: Hostel fees (excluding mess, electricity, water charges) reimbursed upon furnishing a certificate.
Single accommodation in host institution: Rent reimbursed as per actuals up to the ceiling of HRA as per Govt. norms.
Own accommodation: HRA as per city categorization.
benefits.Medical: Access to medical facilities available in the University/Institution/College.
Leave: 30 days per year (in addition to public holidays) with approval from the Head of Department.
Maternity/Paternity leave as per Government of India rules.
Academic leave up to one year without fellowship for teaching or foreign travel for academic work.

documents: Joining Report (prescribed format) signed by Head of Department and countersigned by Registrar.
Contingency utilization certificate (end of each year).

title: Innovation In Science Pursuit For Inspired Research (INSPIRE) - Faculty Fellowship
description: Provides opportunities for 100 young researchers annually with Ph.D. qualifications to carry out independent research in science, technology, medicine, agriculture, pharmacy, and veterinary sciences. The fellowship aims to develop independent scientific profiles and augment high-quality manpower in scientific institutions.
eligibility: Indian citizens or People of Indian Origin with Ph.D. degrees in relevant fields, aged 27-32 years (relaxed for SC/ST/Women up to 37 years, and Divyangjan up to 42 years). Candidates must have a minimum of 60% marks throughout their academic career and publications in reputed journals. Applicants awaiting their Ph.D. award can apply but must secure their degree before selection confirmation.
benefits: Fellowship of ₹1,25,000 per month with an annual increment of ₹2,000 and a Research Grant of ₹7,00,000 per year for five years. The fellowship is taxable as per Indian IT Act.
documents: Passport-size photograph, Class X Mark Sheet or Certificate (proof of Date of Birth), Academic certificates from Class XII onwards, Caste Certificate (if applicable), Letter of appointment (if employed), Professional recognitions and awards, Research experience, Budget breakup for research grants, and Endorsements/letters from host institutes.

title: Junior Research Fellowship (JRF) - Department of Biotechnology (DBT)
description: The DBT Junior Research Fellowship (JRF) program provides financial support to Indian students pursuing doctoral research in biotechnology and life sciences. It is open to candidates with a master's degree in biotechnology or related fields.
eligibility: 1. Applicant must be an Indian citizen. 2. Must have a master's degree in biotechnology or a related field from a recognized university. 3. Should have cleared the GATE or NET exam in biotechnology or a related field. 4. Age limit: 28 years for General and EWS candidates, and 33 years for SC/ST/PH candidates. 5. Must be willing to pursue full-time doctoral research in biotechnology or a related field at a recognized university or research institution in India.
benefits: 1. ₹31,000 per month for the first two years. 2. ₹35,000 per month for the remaining three years.
documents: 1. BET exam scorecard. 2. Research proposal. 3. No Objection Certificate (NOC) from the current employer (if applicable). 4. Passport-size photograph. 5. Signature. 6. Date of birth proof. 7. Academic transcripts for all degrees obtained. 8. Category certificate (if applicable). 9. Persons with Disability (PWD) certificate (if applicable).

title: Scholarships to Students with Disabilities Scheme
description: The scheme, initiated by the Department of Social Security and Women & Child Development, Government of Punjab in 1996, aims to assist students with disabilities in securing education, academic, technical, or professional training to help them earn a living and integrate as contributing members of society. It is fully state-sponsored.
eligibility: 1. Applicant must be a student in Punjab with a disability of 40% or more (as per the RPwD Act 2016). 2. The monthly family/guardian income should not exceed ₹5,000/- per month. 3. The candidate should have secured at least 40% marks in the previous annual examination. 4. Applicable for students with disabilities from 1st class onwards.
benefits: 1. ₹200/- per month for students with disabilities from class 1 to 8. 2. ₹300/- per month for students with disabilities from class 9 and onwards. Note: The scholarship will be credited directly to the student's bank account.
documents: 1. Disability Certificate/ UDID showing 40% or more disability. 2. Income certificate showing annual family income below ₹60,000/-. 3. Copy of bank account details.

title: Stipend to Scheduled Caste Trainees in Technical Training Institutions
description: This scheme, implemented by the Adi Dravidar Welfare and Scheduled Tribes Welfare Department, Union Territory of Puducherry, provides financial support to Scheduled Tribe candidates/trainees undergoing training in Government Technical Training Institutions within the Union Territory of Puducherry.
eligibility: 1. Candidates/trainees undergoing training in the Government Technical Training Institutions of Puducherry. 2. The candidate must belong to the Scheduled Caste community. 3. There is no income limit to apply under the scheme.
benefits: 1. A stipend of ₹170/- per month per trainee for the entire duration of the training.
documents: 1. Identity proof (Aadhaar Card). 2. Passport size photograph. 3. Educational documents (Mark sheets). 4. Caste certificate. 5. Nativity Certificate issued by the Competent Revenue Officials. 6. Bank account details. 7. Any other document, as required.

title: Post Graduate Indira Gandhi Scholarship For Single Girl Child
description: A scholarship scheme by the University Grants Commission (UGC) for girl students admitted to the first year of PG courses in any designated university or postgraduate college, provided they are the only girl child in their family. The scheme aims to support the postgraduate education of single girl children and promote the value of small family norms.
eligibility: 1. The applicant must be a citizen of India. 2. The applicant must be the only girl child in the family. 3. Girl students without brothers or who are twin/fraternal daughters may apply. 4. The age of the applicant should not exceed 30 years at the time of admission to the first year of the PG course. 5. The applicant must have taken admission to a regular, full-time first-year Master’s Degree course in a designated university or postgraduate college. 6. The course must be non-professional, and the student must attend regular classes. 7. Students pursuing education through distance mode are not eligible.
benefits: 1. ₹36,200/- per annum for two years, i.e., the full duration of the PG course. 2. 3,000 fresh scholarships are awarded annually and disbursed through DBT mode.

By Student: Proof of admission into a recognized Master’s degree course in an institute located in India recognized by the UGC.
Affidavit regarding single girl child status (Annexure-II format).
By Institution: Progress Report after completion of the first year along with the percentage of marks obtained.
Funds Utilization Certificate after completion of the first year.

title: Award of Opportunity Cost to Parents of Scheduled Caste Girl Students
description: Implemented by the Adi Dravidar Welfare Department, Government of Puducherry, this scheme aims to financially assist Scheduled Caste parents for sending their daughters to school. It provides opportunity cost compensation to eliminate child labor and encourage education among Scheduled Caste girl students.
eligibility: 1. The student must be a girl studying in Class VI to XII in a Government or Government-recognized institution in Puducherry. 2. The student must belong to the Scheduled Caste community. 3. The girl student and her parent/guardian must be a native of Puducherry by birth or continuous residence for at least 3 years. 4. The annual income of the parents should not exceed ₹24,000. 5. Opportunity cost is payable only if the girl student has been promoted to the next higher class.
benefits: ₹3,000/- per academic year is payable to the parents of Scheduled Caste girls studying in Classes VI to XII. This amount is provided in addition to the Pre-Matric Scholarship. The opportunity cost is not payable for re-admitted girls repeating a class due to failure or discontinuance.
documents: Residence-cum-nativity certificate issued by the competent authority

title: India-Afghanistan Fellowship Programme
description: Started in 2010-11, the India-Afghanistan Fellowship Programme supports agricultural human resource development in Afghanistan. It offers fellowships for Afghan nationals to pursue Bachelor's, Master's, and Ph.D. degrees in Indian Agricultural Universities (AUs) in agriculture and allied sciences.

eligibility.General Requirement: Good knowledge of English for all programmes.

Bachelor's Degree Programme.Academic Qualification: Passed 10+2 examination with Physics, Chemistry, and Biology/Mathematics or both.

Master's Degree Programme.Academic Qualification: Four-year Bachelor's degree in agriculture or related subjects.
Additional Requirements: For M.V.Sc.: Passed B.V.Sc. & A.H. with internship under a 5-year degree programme.
For M.Tech.: Passed B.Tech. or B.E. programme.
Minimum OGPA: 5.50/10 or equivalent percentage of marks at the Bachelor's degree level.

Doctoral Programme.Academic Qualification: Master's degree in the respective or related subject.
Doctoral Programme.Minimum OGPA: 6.5/10 or equivalent percentage of marks at the Master's degree level.

Fellowship Amount.Bachelor's Degree: INR 12,000 per month for four years.
Fellowship Amount.Master's Degree: INR 12,000 per month for two years.
Fellowship Amount.Ph.D. Programme: INR 15,000 per month for three years.

Other Benefits: Provision of research contingency grant: INR 6,000/year for M.Sc. and INR 10,000/year for Ph.D.
Annual bench fee (USD $2000 per year) covered under the India-Afghanistan Fund.
benefits.Expenditure Coverage: Tuition fees, hostel fees, mess, medical, and travel within India are covered using the consolidated fellowship amount.

title: Young Interns Program (YIP)
description: An internship program by the Government of Rajasthan providing opportunities for youth to gain practical experience in government programs, schemes, and projects.
eligibility: Applicants aged 21 to 30 with postgraduate qualifications (minimum 60% marks) or graduates in Engineering, Medical, Law, CA, or CS streams with minimum 60% marks.
benefits: ₹30,000 per month stipend, ₹2,500 per month for laptop and internet support, 12 casual leaves annually.
documents: Photograph, Letter of Intent, Domicile Certificate of Rajasthan, Proof of Age, Educational Qualification Certificates, Proof of Experience (if any), Bank Account Details

title: Technology Development & Utilization Programme for Women (TDUPW)
description: A program by DSIR to promote technology development, utilization, and capability building specifically for women.

For Projects: Studies/surveys for assessing technology-related information needs of women.
Documentation and content development on technologies beneficial to women, including food processing, water conservation, health, and hygiene.
Establishing consultancy cells for imparting technical knowledge.
Awareness creation and training for women in production activities and community management.
Case studies of successful R&D, technology development, and businesswomen.
For Institutions: Organizations in the government, non-government, or professional sectors working on women's technology development.

Institutions receiving annual grants from central/state government agencies, universities, R&D institutions, public sector undertakings, or registered under the Societies Registration Act, Companies Act, or Indian Trusts Act.

benefits.Duration: Time-bound projects, typically under two years.
Quantum of Financial Assistance: Financial support (partial or full) for expenses such as manpower, consumables, travel within the country, and miscellaneous expenditures.
Assistance excludes infrastructure and building costs.
benefits.Release of Funds: Released in instalments based on milestones achieved and formalities like progress reports and utilization certificates.

documents: Letter of forwarding the project proposal and undertaking from the institution head.
Certificate of Responsibility and Commitment from Investigators.
Technical and financial details of the proposal.
For non-government institutions: Registration Certificate, Annual Report (last 3 years), Auditor’s Report (last 3 years).

title: Top Class Education For Students With Disabilities
description: A scholarship scheme by DoEPwDs for Students with Disabilities (SwDs) pursuing studies beyond class 12th in a full-time course in notified institutions. It aims to support SwDs in pursuing higher education and achieving a dignified place in society.

Student: Admitted in a full-time course in a notified institution.
Percentage of disability should be 40% or above.
Pursuing a Graduate or Post Graduate Degree or Diploma.
Total annual income of Parent/Guardian should be ≤ ₹6.00 Lakh.
Not more than two children with disabilities in the same family can avail of the scholarship.

benefits.Tuition Fee: Full tuition fee and non-refundable charges (Ceiling of Rs. 2.00 LPA per student for private institutions).
benefits.Special Allowance: ₹ 2000 per month (related to disability types like Reader, Escort, Helper Allowance, etc.).

title: Innovation In Science Pursuit For Inspired Research (INSPIRE) Fellowship
description: The INSPIRE Fellowship is part of the INSPIRE scheme by the Department of Science and Technology (DST), Government of India, aimed at attracting students to pursue doctoral degrees in basic and applied sciences, including engineering, medicine, agriculture, veterinary sciences, and pharmacy.

Primary Eligibility: 1st Rank Holders in any recognized University/Institution in PG level examinations in Basic/Applied Sciences, Engineering, Pharmacy, Agricultural Sciences, Veterinary Sciences.
1st Rank Holders in Medicine at Graduate/Post-Graduate level conducted by an Indian University/Institution.
INSPIRE Scholars with at least 70% marks (or CGPA equivalent) in M.Sc./Integrated M.S./M.Sc. or equivalent.
Additional Notes: Fulfilling eligibility criteria does not guarantee final fellowship award.
Selection based on academic merit, research proposal, supervisor’s credentials, and host institution.

benefits.Duration: Up to five years from the Effective Date or completion of the Ph.D. program, whichever is earlier.
benefits.HRA: Applicable as per GoI norms depending on the location of the Host Institute.
benefits.Contingency Expenditure: ₹20,000 per annum with a maximum ceiling of ₹20,000 for the unspent balance adjusted to the next year.
benefits.Internship: Short-term Research Internship (up to 18 months) at any national or international lab/university/institute during the 5-year tenure.
benefits.Upgradation: Fellowship can be upgraded from JRF to SRF after 2 years, or 1 year if research paper acceptance is received.
benefits.Payment Mode: Transferred through PFMS to the student’s host institute’s ZBSA Bank Account, which then transfers the fellowship to the student's account.

title: Pre Matric Scholarship for Students with Disabilities
description: This scholarship is for students with disabilities studying in Class 9th or 10th in a government-recognized school. The scheme is aimed at providing financial support to students with disabilities to continue their education and find opportunities for a dignified life.

eligibility.Age & Class: The applicant must be studying in Class 9th or 10th.
eligibility.Disability: The applicant must have a disability of 40% or more.
eligibility.Income Limit: The total annual income of the parents/guardians should not exceed ₹2.00 Lakh.
eligibility.Exclusivity: The applicant must not be availing of any other government scholarship, and must not be the third sibling receiving this scholarship.

title: Prime Minister’s Scholarship Scheme (PMSS) for Railway Protection Force (RPF)
description: Introduced by the Hon’ble Prime Minister of India in 2005 and implemented from 2008-09, the PMSS is aimed at encouraging higher technical and professional education for the dependent wards of Ex/Serving RPF/RPSF personnel and widows (below the rank of Gazetted officer).

eligibility.Applicants: Dependent wards of Ex/Serving RPF/RPSF personnel and widows (below the rank of Gazetted officer).
eligibility.Course Limitations: Only regular courses from institutes/universities/colleges with AISHE recognition. No distance learning courses.
Courses Eligible: Other Government Recognized Professional Degree Courses
eligibility.Nationality: Students studying abroad are not eligible.

benefits.Special Letter: Letter from the Hon’ble Prime Minister
benefits.Duration: Two to five years as per the duration of the course

title: Free Education for Sports Medal Winners / Participants of National/International Events
description: This scheme provides financial assistance to medal winners and elite sports persons who participate in national or international sporting events and are studying in full-time regular courses at recognized universities/colleges. The objective is to motivate sports persons to achieve higher levels of performance by assisting them financially for education and sports development.

eligibility.Eligible Candidates: Medal winners in national games or participants in recognized international sporting events.
Institutions: Universities/Institutions/Colleges under sections 2(f) and 12(b) of the UGC Act, 1956.
Deemed to be Universities under section 3 of the UGC Act, 1956.
eligibility.Subsequent Eligibility: Scholarship eligibility in subsequent years depends on the student’s performance in both sports and academics.

Olympics/World Championships (more than.Allowances for Special Courses Abroad: As per Government of India’s norms

Olympics/World Championships (less than.Allowances for Special Courses Abroad: As per Government of India’s norms

Asian Championships, World University Ga.Allowances for Special Courses Abroad: As per Government of India’s norms

National Level Competitions, SAF Games.Allowances for Special Courses Abroad: As per Government of India’s norms

title: Indian Agricultural Research Institute - Scholarship
description: The scholarship provides financial support to students enrolled in M.Sc. and Ph.D. courses at the Indian Agricultural Research Institute (IARI). The awards are based on merit and performance at the Post Graduate School, with a duration of up to two years for M.Sc. students and three years for Ph.D. students. Extensions may be granted in exceptional cases.

M.Sc./M.Tech. (Agricultural Engineering).Educational Qualification: Bachelor's degree under 10+2+4, 10+2+3, or 10+1+4 system. B.Sc. degree under 10+2+2 system prior to 1985.

Ph.D. Courses.Minimum Marks Required: 60% or OGPA of 7.50 out of 10 for General/OBC candidates, 55% or OGPA of 7.00 out of 10 for SC/ST/PC candidates.

General Eligibility.Nationality: Indian nationals or persons domiciled in India
General Eligibility.Age Limit: Minimum age for Ph.D. admission: 21 years as of 31st July of the year of admission
General Eligibility.Other Criteria: Fellows must devote full time to study and cannot hold another paid or unpaid appointment.

In-Service Candidates.Stipend Adjustment: Scholarship amount adjusted to ₹3,000 per month in addition to full leave salary for in-service candidates on study leave.

title: Scholarship to 2 Years Pre-Service Student Teachers undergoing D.El.Ed Programme at DIETs and CTTC Sohra
description: Launched by the Department of Education, Government of Meghalaya in April 2012, this scholarship aims to attract the best talent to pursue teaching as a profession. It provides financial support to students undertaking the D.El.Ed programme at DIETs and CTTC Sohra, with a focus on improving the quality of education in rural areas.

eligibility.Age Limit: 18 to 27 years (Relaxation of 5 years for SC/ST/OBC/PWD candidates).

Educational Qualifications.Class XII: Passed or equivalent examination with 50% marks (5% relaxation for ST/SC/OBC/PWD).
Educational Qualifications.Waiting for Results: Candidates awaiting Class XII results can apply, with the condition to produce provisional/original documents during counseling.

eligibility.Other Criteria: Must be interested in becoming a teacher.

title: Internship Scheme For PG/Research Students In Directorate General Of Trade Remedies (DGTR)
